title: T11: fabric doctor --apply-lint safety prompt
description: Q-17 resolved: `--apply-lint` mutates frontmatters + does `git mv` operations without safety prompt, so users won't run it confidently. Before any mutations: print mutation plan (N frontmatter mutations, M file moves with from→to mapping) then `clack.confirm`: 'About to mutate N frontmatters + git mv M files. Proceed? [y/N]'. Default N (safety-first). Skip via `--yes` flag OR `FABRIC_NONINTERACTIVE=1` env var. Document the env var + flag in `docs/configuration.md` (created/extended by T7).

files.change: in --apply-lint code path: BEFORE any frontmatter write or git mv: (1) compute and print mutation plan (counts + per-file from→to for moves), (2) check `--yes` flag and `FABRIC_NONINTERACTIVE` env var — if either set, skip prompt; otherwise call clack.confirm with default false; (3) exit code 1 if user declines or stdin non-tty without --yes
add --yes flag to doctor command parser (boolean, default false)
extend T7 doc with new section '--apply-lint safety': document --yes flag behavior + FABRIC_NONINTERACTIVE=1 env var + recommend CI usage with --yes

tests: (a) interactive tty + user N → exit 1 no mutations; (b) interactive tty + user Y → mutations proceed; (c) non-tty stdin without --yes → exit 1; (d) non-tty stdin with --yes → mutations proceed; (e) FABRIC_NONINTERACTIVE=1 without --yes → mutations proceed
add assertion: `fabric doctor --apply-lint` invoked with non-tty stdin and no --yes exits 1; with --yes exits 0 (or whatever the doctor exit is for clean run)

implementation: Use existing clack import in doctor.ts (or add if missing) for confirm prompt

Compute mutation plan during lint scan phase (already produces the list of issues + intended fixes); render as human-readable preview: 'Frontmatter mutations: N
  - canonical/foo.md: maturity=draft → endorsed
  ...
File moves: M
  - canonical/bar.md → canonical/baz.md
  ...'
Add --yes boolean flag to doctor command argument parser; check process.env.FABRIC_NONINTERACTIVE for === '1'
Branch: if (--yes || FABRIC_NONINTERACTIVE=1) skip prompt; else if (!stdin.isTTY) exit 1 with error 'stdin not interactive and --yes not set'; else clack.confirm(default false)
Update docs/configuration.md (from T7) with new section
Write unit tests covering all 5 branches
Coverage gate: shell out to doctor with non-tty stdin in both with/without --yes modes

reference.pattern: clack.confirm pattern from other CLI commands; argparse pattern in doctor.ts
reference.examples: Default-N safety pattern: `clack.confirm({ message, initialValue: false })`

criteria: GIVEN `fabric doctor --apply-lint` invoked in interactive terminal WHEN lint identifies mutations THEN stdout shows mutation plan with explicit counts AND clack.confirm prompts with default N (no auto-proceed)
GIVEN `fabric doctor --apply-lint --yes` invoked with no tty WHEN lint identifies mutations THEN mutations proceed without prompt AND exit code 0 (on success)
GIVEN `fabric doctor --apply-lint` invoked with no tty AND no --yes flag AND no FABRIC_NONINTERACTIVE env var WHEN command runs THEN exit code 1 with stderr error message explaining requirement
GIVEN `FABRIC_NONINTERACTIVE=1 fabric doctor --apply-lint` (no --yes flag) WHEN command runs THEN mutations proceed without prompt
docs/configuration.md has a section documenting --yes flag and FABRIC_NONINTERACTIVE env var

rationale.chosen_approach: Default-N clack.confirm + dual bypass mechanisms (flag + env var) — safety first, two escape hatches for automation (CI flag, env var for nested invocations)
alternatives_considered: Always require --yes (no prompt) — UX regression for interactive use
Dry-run flag only — doesn't solve the safety-prompt problem
decision_factors: Q-17 resolution explicitly requires confirm + skip via flag OR env var
Non-tty without --yes exit-1 prevents accidental CI mutation
rationale.tradeoffs: Two bypass mechanisms — slight surface increase, but both are documented and orthogonal (flag for explicit CLI, env var for nested process)

manual_checks: Run `fabric doctor --apply-lint` in terminal → see mutation plan and N-default prompt
success_metrics: Zero accidental mutations from non-tty invocations without --yes (covered by exit 1)
Default-N prompt visible in 100% of interactive --apply-lint runs
unit: 5 branches covered: interactive Y/N, non-tty with/without --yes, env var bypass
integration: Coverage gate shell-out test: non-tty + --apply-lint without --yes exits 1
